channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Justice Department seeks 7 year sentence for George Santos
item.description: Prosecutors said the length of the requested sentence — 87 months — reflects the serious nature of Santos’s “unparalleled” crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Sec Hegseth to visit Panama after Trump's demands for canal's return
item.description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth will visit Panama next week for meetings with the country's leaders, following remarks from President Donald Trump about potentially acquiring the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prosecutors seek 7 years in prison for disgraced ex-US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: Prosecutors are seeking more than seven years in prison for disgraced former congressman George Santos after he pleaded guilty to federal fraud and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Maya Angelou memoir, Holocaust book are among those pulled from Naval Academy library in DEI purge
item.description: Books on the Holocaust, histories of feminism, civil rights and racism, and Maya Angelou’s famous autobiography, “I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings,” were among the nearly 400 volumes removed from the U.S. Naval Academy’s library this week.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: School board leader: It's too early to roll back universal free meals program
item.description: A bill moving its way through the Minnesota Legislature seeks to adjust the popular but expensive universal school meals program passed in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Chinese-owned GNC stores operating on US military bases spark national security concerns
item.description: Rep. Pat Harrigan warns GNC’s Chinese ownership poses a national security threat as the company operates 85 stores on U.S. military bases without disclosing ownership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth is to travel to Panama for meetings following Trump's demands for the canal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will travel to Panama next week to meet with leaders of the Central American country following recent Trump administration allegations of Chinese interference in the operations of the critical Panama Canal shipping lane.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thune faces GOP divisions on critical budget resolution
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) faces a number of divisions among Senate Republicans that could derail the Senate budget resolution, a measure that will be critical to passing President Trump’s legislative agenda later this year.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Security Agency chief Timothy Haugh, deputy fired
item.description: Timothy Haugh, the head of the NSA, was among the top staffers fired from one of the nation's top intelligence agencies
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Obama urges Americans to 'possibly sacrifice' in resisting Trump policies
item.description: Former President Barack Obama told a crowd of people on Thursday night that they might need to "possibly sacrifice" in resisting President Donald Trump's policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The job market remains incredibly healthy -- but the tariff storm could upend things
item.description: Employers added 228,000 jobs in March, showcasing a solid labor market. But uncertainty over tariffs and tepid consumer spending may weigh on job growth in the months to come.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: Florida lawmakers want to ease high school graduation requirements
item.description: State lawmakers are working to ease the anxiety of taking a test to help students earn their high school diploma. This includes math and English exams.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PHOTOS: Biden’s ‘ambitious climate goals’ go down in literal flames by popular American beach
item.description: A major part of a first-of-its-kind green energy project, touted by Biden administration, is now lying in ruins and polluting some of America’s beautiful ocean and seashore in Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How we got to Liberation Day: A look at Trump’s past comments on tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump has routinely declared tariffs as one of the most beautiful words in the dictionary and has regularly accused foreign countries of ripping off the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: HHS staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those who were fired from the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), a now former employee told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Pentagon inspector general announces investigation into Hegseth’s use of Signal
item.description: The announcement comes after lawmakers voiced concern about whether top national security officials discussed classified information on Signal about a military strike against Houthi rebels in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Five big questions the GOP's budget plan for Trump leaves unanswered
item.description: Senate Republicans are pushing to adopt a budget blueprint this week to kick off a complex process to advance President Trump’s tax agenda.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump fires NSC officials a day after far-right activist raises concerns to him about staff loyalty
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Thursday that he's fired "some" White House National Security Council officials, a move that comes a day after far-right activist Laura Loomer raised concerns directly to him about staff loyalty. Trump downplayed Loomer's influence on the firings.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Coincidence? Recession-era artists releasing new music
item.description: "Recession pop" refers to the type of dance-floor jams that were popular around 2008 when the U.S. economy imploded.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Despite backlash, Florida county term limit bill advances in the House
item.description: Florida voters may have the chance to set term limits, not just for state lawmakers, but also for county commissioners and school board members.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those fired from the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Auditor reports that Minnesota muni liquor store profits up, in part due to THC offerings
item.description: Minnesota State Auditor Julie Blaha issued her annual report on municipal-run liquor store sales. It was the first include sales of hemp-derived THC beverages and gummies, and operators suggested those items contributed to store profits in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Potential Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: new analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal judge drops corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: Adams was scheduled to go on trial in April until new leadership at the Justice Department under the Trump administration ordered prosecutors in New York in February to drop the case, sparking a public outcry and resignations of prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas and Carrie Johnson, MPR News

item.title: Proposed cut in local aid to prevent aquatic invasive species sparks fears of more infested lakes
item.description: For the past decade, Minnesota has distributed about $10 million a year to counties for aquatic invasive species prevention. Gov. Tim Walz’s proposed budget reduced that amount by 50 percent. Some lake advocates worry cutting the funding could potentially lead to more infested waters.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams federal bribery case dismissed
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams' federal bribery case has been dismissed, court records show. In a letter filed Wednesday, Judge Dale Ho ordered the case dropped with prejudice, meaning the government cannot bring these charges against Adams again in the future.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: Susan Crawford, a Dane County judge who led legal fights to protect union power and abortion rights and to oppose voter ID, defeated Republican-backed Brad Schimel in a race that broke records for spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: State Sen. Napoleon Harris wins Thornton Township supervisor seat, Jason House victorious in Dolton mayoral race
item.description: Village of Dolton Senior Trustee Jason House won the election for Dolton mayor by a landslide, while State Sen. Napoleon B. Harris III claimed victory to become the Thornton Township Supervisor-elect, unseating the incumbent, self-proclaimed “Super Mayor” Tiffany Henyard.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, WGN 9

item.title: GOP retains two House seats in Florida, Democrats claim 'historic' improvement
item.description: Republicans won special elections in two Florida Congressional districts. The margins of victory in the heavily-Republican districts were significantly narrower than in November.
item.dc:creator: Greg Allen, MPR News

item.title: New York City mayoral candidates vow to address rising antisemitism
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The leading candidates for New York City mayor say they have plans to fight a rise in antisemitism. One in 10 New Yorkers is Jewish, making it the largest Jewish population outside of Israel. Former Gov.
item.dc:creator: Anthony DiLorenzo, PIX 11

item.title: Election Day: Suburban voters head to the polls
item.description: Several elections will take place in Chicago-area suburbs on Tuesday, including a notable one in Dolton where incumbent Tiffany Henyard will run against Jason House, who won the primary election in February.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WGN 9

item.title: Luna accuses House Freedom Caucus of smear; exits group over parental proxy voting battle
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) announced Monday that she is leaving the House Freedom Caucus amid her battle with GOP leadership and hard-line Republicans over her push to allow proxy voting for new parents.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘not joking’ about a third term. Republicans insist he is
item.description: Congressional Republicans are brushing off President Trump’s latest comments about seeking a third term, downplaying them as a joke designed to needle the media even as the president insists he is serious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House to remove magnolia tree dating to Andrew Jackson
item.description: Trump added that White House staff will preserve the Magnolia tree’s “Historic wood,” which, Trump said, “may be used for other high and noble purposes!!!”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. DeSantis proposes $1K rebate checks to cover Florida homeowners' property tax
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a proposal on Monday that would give Florida homeowners a rebate averaging $1,000 for each homesteaded property in the state.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘very angry’ and ‘pissed off’ at Putin during NBC News interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was “very angry” and “pissed off” when Russian President Vladimir Putin criticized the credibility of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s leadership, adding that the comments were “not going in the right location.
item.dc:creator: Kristin Welker, WFLA, NBC
